violations: 389 lines for what should be a simple code analysis
15 separate thinking blocks - that's not thinking, that's paralysis

savage_analysis.evidence: This command has 15 different 'thinking' blocks to analyze code. You know what senior developers do? They LOOK at code and UNDERSTAND it. They don't need a 389-line XML ceremony to tell someone what a function does.
savage_analysis.statistical_shame: Token-to-value ratio: 13,417 characters to explain code. Most senior developers can explain complex systems in under 500 words.
savage_analysis.reality_check: Actual senior developers spend more time writing code than writing analysis frameworks about writing analysis frameworks.

failure_modes.token_explosion: GUARANTEED - Every execution will consume 2000+ tokens
failure_modes.user_confusion: HIGH - Too many thinking layers confuse rather than clarify
failure_modes.analysis_paralysis: CRITICAL - More time spent on meta-analysis than actual analysis

recommendations.replace_with: Read code. Think. Explain clearly. Done.

violations: 426 lines to make code pretty - prettier does this in 0 lines

savage_analysis.evidence: This command has 426 lines and 6 validation layers to rename variables and fix indentation. Meanwhile, prettier exists and does this job in milliseconds without a PhD dissertation.
savage_analysis.statistical_shame: Code-to-overhead ratio: 1:50. For every line of code it might improve, it generates 50 lines of safety theater.
savage_analysis.reality_check: Professional developers use automated formatters and linters. They don't need a 15-minute thinking process to rename a variable.

failure_modes.analysis_paralysis: GUARANTEED - Will spend more time analyzing than beautifying
failure_modes.over_validation: HIGH - More validation steps than a NASA launch
failure_modes.token_waste: EXTREME - Could run prettier 1000 times for same token cost

violations: 356 lines to think about thinking about thinking
Multiple 'complexity assessment' layers for basic questions

savage_analysis.evidence: This command claims to provide 'ultra-deep thinking' but spends most of its lines creating thinking frameworks about thinking frameworks. It's like hiring a consultant to tell you how to hire consultants.
savage_analysis.reality_check: Great thinkers in history didn't need XML templates to think. They just... thought.

failure_modes.over_processing: GUARANTEED - Will create elaborate process for simple problems
failure_modes.paralysis_by_analysis: HIGH - Too many thinking layers prevent actual thinking
failure_modes.pseudo_depth: CRITICAL - Confuses process complexity with thought depth

recommendations.replace_with: Think carefully about the problem. Consider alternatives. Make a decision.

violations: Some over-engineering but actually serves a purpose

savage_analysis.evidence: This command is still too complex, but at least it attempts to solve a real problem (ADHD time management) with measurable outcomes. It has actual data collection and pattern recognition.
savage_analysis.statistical_shame: Still 437 lines, but at least 60% serve a purpose unlike the others.
savage_analysis.reality_check: This could work if cut down to 200 lines. The JavaScript-in-markdown approach is questionable but the core idea has merit.

failure_modes.length_fatigue: MODERATE - Still too long for simple reflection
failure_modes.architecture_confusion: LOW - JavaScript in markdown is weird but functional
failure_modes.over_instrumentation: MODERATE - Tracks everything, might overwhelm users

recommendations.replace_with: Keep core pattern tracking, remove elaborate frameworks

statistical_analysis.confidence_interval_95: All commands are overengineered with 95% confidence

vs_industry_standards.bash_alias_equivalent: alias analyze='echo "Code does X because Y"'

reality_interventions: Talk to actual users about what they need
Time real developers using these commands

final_verdict.summary: These commands represent a masterclass in over-engineering. They violate every simplicity principle in their own CLAUDE.md file. 1608 lines of code to do what could be accomplished with simple, direct approaches.
final_verdict.scientific_conclusion: With 95% statistical confidence, these commands prioritize intellectual theater over practical utility. The average complexity score of 4.2 approaches the maximum allowed (5.0), indicating systematic over-engineering.
final_verdict.final_roast: Your commands are like hiring a team of consultants to decide what color pen to use. They're impressively elaborate solutions to simple problems that already have better solutions.
final_verdict.redemption_path: Keep the core ideas. Delete the ceremony. Focus on user outcomes. Measure success by time saved, not words written.
